armed-conflicts.headline: Multiple conflicts escalate; Israel strikes Lebanon, Russia drones Ukraine
armed-conflicts.summary: January 25, 2026 saw continued and escalating armed conflicts globally. Israeli airstrikes targeted four crossing points along the Syria-Lebanon border, reportedly used by Hezbollah to smuggle weapons, resulting in at least two deaths and 20 injuries in southern Lebanon. Meanwhile, Russia launched approximately 400 drones in overnight attacks across Ukraine, striking major cities and energy infrastructure, causing significant disruptions in Kyiv and Chernihiv. Ongoing conflict in Sudan also continued to generate the world's largest displacement crisis, with widespread civilian harm and tactics of siege and starvation employed.

human-rights.headline: Iran crackdown deaths confirmed; UNRWA facilities hit in Gaza
human-rights.summary: On January 25, 2026, newly reviewed documents revealed over 36,500 people were killed in Iran's January 8-9 crackdown on nationwide protests, with reports of security forces detaining wounded patients from hospitals. In the Gaza Strip, UNRWA reported that an UNRWA health center in Jabalia Camp was directly struck by Israeli forces' tank shelling, and an UNRWA school in Khan Younis was indirectly impacted by an explosive bullet. Additionally, the Inter-American Court of Human Rights declared Honduras responsible for discriminatory arrests and failing to investigate the death of a trans woman.

climate-events.headline: North America gripped by historic winter storm; Mozambique floods displace 600k
climate-events.summary: A massive and exceptionally rare winter storm affected large parts of the central and eastern United States and southeastern Canada on January 24-25, 2026, bringing severe snowfall, freezing rain, and strong winds, leading to widespread power outages for over a million customers and thousands of flight cancellations. In Mozambique, severe flooding displaced approximately 600,000 people and destroyed infrastructure, prompting an appeal for international assistance. Meanwhile, Afghanistan reported heavy rainfall and snowfall causing 61 deaths.

public-health.headline: Sudan faces severe health crisis; US exits WHO
public-health.summary: Sudan's ongoing conflict has created a catastrophic public health situation, with 21.0 million people requiring health assistance amidst an overstretched health system, low immunization coverage, and high malnutrition rates. Over 40,000 injuries have been reported, with famine conditions confirmed in some areas. In a significant global health shift, the United States officially withdrew from the World Health Organization on January 25, 2026, citing concerns over the agency's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic.

economic-crises.headline: Global growth fragile amidst geopolitical uncertainty; US tariffs threatened
economic-crises.summary: The global economy is projected to see steady but fragile growth in 2026, around 3%, supported by easier monetary policy, fiscal stimulus, and the AI boom, but facing significant downside risks and elevated geopolitical uncertainty. The US economy is projected to expand by 2.4% in 2026, but President Trump threatened 100% tariffs on Canadian goods if Ottawa reaches a trade agreement with Beijing. Libya saw strong interest in its first oil and natural gas exploration licensing round in 17 years.

democratic-erosion.headline: Iran crackdown continues; US election interference concerns mount
democratic-erosion.summary: Iran continued to experience a severe crackdown on anti-government protests, with reports of thousands killed and human rights activists expressing alarm over the withholding of detainee numbers and lack of legal representation. In the United States, Democratic state election chiefs were reportedly meeting privately to plan responses to potential election interference by President Donald Trump in the upcoming midterms, including concerns about armed federal agents at polling places or disruptions to mail-in voting. Brazil, however, achieved a notable democratic milestone by convicting former President Jair Bolsonaro of attempting a coup.

humanitarian.headline: Sudan famine worsens; Mozambique floods displace 600,000
humanitarian.summary: Sudan is grappling with a catastrophic humanitarian crisis, with nearly 25 million people facing acute food insecurity and famine conditions confirmed in El Fasher and Kadugli. The World Food Programme warned of imminent pipeline breaks and ration cuts starting in January 2026 without urgent funding, as 33.7 million people require assistance in 2026. In Mozambique, severe flooding displaced over 600,000 people and destroyed local infrastructure, leading the government to appeal for international assistance.
